2604.10410.title: CWCD: Category-Wise Contrastive Decoding for Structured Medical Report Generation
2604.10410.paper_summary: Interpreting chest X-rays is inherently challenging due to the overlap between anatomical structures and the subtle presentation of many clinically significant pathologies, making accurate diagnosis time-consuming even for experienced radiologists. Recent radiology-focused foundation models, such as LLaVA-Rad and Maira-2, have positioned multi-modal large language models (MLLMs) at the forefront of automated radiology report generation (RRG). However, despite these advances, current foundation models generate reports in a single forward pass. This decoding strategy diminishes attention to visual tokens and increases reliance on language priors as generation proceeds, which in turn introduces spurious pathology co-occurrences in the generated reports. To mitigate these limitations, we propose Category-Wise Contrastive Decoding (CWCD), a novel and modular framework designed to enhance structured radiology report generation (SRRG). Our approach introduces category-specific parameterization and generates category-wise reports by contrasting normal X-rays with masked X-rays using category-specific visual prompts. Experimental results demonstrate that CWCD consistently outperforms baseline methods across both clinical efficacy and natural language generation metrics. An ablation study further elucidates the contribution of each architectural component to overall performance.
2604.10410.paper_summary_zh: 解讀胸部X光片本質上具有挑戰性，因為解剖結構之間的重疊以及許多臨床上重要病理的微妙表現，使得即使是經驗豐富的放射科醫生也需要耗費時間來進行準確診斷。最近專注於放射學的基礎模型，如LLaVA-Rad和Maira-2，已將多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）置於自動放射學報告生成（RRG）的最前沿。然而，儘管有這些進展，目前的基礎模型仍然在單次前向傳遞中生成報告。這種解碼策略減少了對視覺標記的注意，並在生成過程中增加了對語言先驗的依賴，這反過來又在生成的報告中引入了虛假的病理共現。為了減輕這些限制，我們提出了類別-wise對比解碼（CWCD），這是一個新穎且模組化的框架，旨在增強結構化放射學報告生成（SRRG）。我們的方法引入了類別特定的參數化，並通過使用類別特定的視覺提示，將正常X光片與遮蔽的X光片進行對比，生成類別-wise報告。實驗結果顯示，CWCD在臨床效能和自然語言生成指標上始終優於基線方法。一項消融研究進一步闡明了每個架構組件對整體性能的貢獻。
2604.10410.author: Shantam Srivastava et.al.
2604.10410.authors: Shantam Srivastava, Mahesh Bhosale, David Doermann, Mingchen Gao
